description: A declaration that a translation, verification, or adapter is wanted, with terms. `constraints` is a structured ThConstraint tagged-union set and `eligibility` is a ThEligibility combinator tree; reward.summary stays narrative, externalRef stays the machine-actionable hook. Per P5, the project does not intermediate fulfillment: payment, review, and acceptance happen on external rails referenced here.

main.description: A request primitive. Someone states what they want (a lens, a verification, an adapter) and the structured constraints + eligibility they require; fulfillment is resolved off-substrate.

wants.description: What is being requested. Exactly one of: a lens between two schemas, a verification of a lens, or an adapter for a framework.

constraints.description: Structured constraints the deliverable must satisfy. Matching is structural: consumers dispatch on the tagged variant.

reward.description: Reward terms. The bounty record does not transact; `externalRef` is the machine-actionable hook; `summary` is narrative.

externalRef.description: URL pointing to the rail that handles actual reward (grant portal, payment platform, attestation service).

eligibility.description: Structured eligibility predicate in postfix-operator form: each item is either an atomic predicate or a combinator (and/or/not) that pops its operands from the running stack. An empty array means 'no eligibility restriction'. A single atomic item is the lone predicate.

fulfillment.description: Once fulfilled, points to the deliverable record (lens, verification, or adapter).

status.description: Open-enum lifecycle slug declared by the requester. Resolved against `statusVocab` when present.

basis.description: Grounding for the bouletic attitude. Omit when the repo owner is the requester (self-asserted). Set explicitly when a third party is attributing the wanting to another party — the basis records on what grounds the attribution is made (community policy, an off-atproto signal, or derivation from another record).

bidirectional.description: Whether the requested lens must be invertible.

kind.description: Open-enum verification-kind slug. Resolved against `kindVocab` when present, otherwise against the canonical idiolect verification-kinds vocabulary.

constraintPerformance.description: Quantitative performance bound (latency, throughput, error rate) the deliverable must meet.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold. Comparison direction is carried by `cmp`.

cmp.description: How `metric` relates to `threshold`. Default 'le' if omitted.

constraintConformance.description: A verification kind (and optionally a specific property) the deliverable must pass.

kind.description: Open-enum verification-kind slug. Resolved against `kindVocab` when present.

property.description: Optional specific property the verification must establish.

constraintLicense.description: License requirement expressed as an SPDX expression plus optional allow/deny lists.

spdx.description: SPDX expression the deliverable must satisfy (e.g. 'MIT OR Apache-2.0').

constraintDeadline.description: Datetime deadline with optional grace window.

constraintDependency.description: Another bounty this one waits on. Claims are ineligible until the dependency's status is `fulfilled`.

eligibilityMember.description: Atomic predicate: claimer is a member of the named community.

eligibilityVerificationFor.description: Atomic predicate: claimer has published a verification for the named lens property.

eligibilityDid.description: Atomic predicate: claimer's DID matches exactly.

eligibilityAnd.description: Combinator: conjoin the top two predicates on the evaluation stack.

eligibilityOr.description: Combinator: disjoin the top two predicates on the evaluation stack.

eligibilityNot.description: Combinator: negate the top predicate on the evaluation stack.
